channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Leveraged Bitcoin Rally Faces U.S. Demand Drought Amid Inflation Fears
item.description: Bitcoin's surge past $80,000 is driven by leveraged offshore futures trading, not strong U.S. spot demand, as shown by the negative Coinbase Premium. On-chain data indicates less spot buying and more derivative activity. High U.S. inflation fears, fueled by a surprising PPI report, have pressured Bitcoin, causing it to fall below key support levels and raising doubts about the rally's lasting power. While upcoming regulatory votes could bring clarity, current market sentiment is cautious.

item.title: Fasset Raises $51M to Expand Stablecoin Services in Emerging Markets
item.description: Los Angeles-based Fasset has closed a $51 million Series B funding round, with participation from SBI Group, Investcorp, and Arz Portföy. The capital will fuel expansion into new markets and enhance its stablecoin payment infrastructure, targeting over 50 corridors across Asia, Africa, and the Middle East. Fasset leverages stablecoins for faster, lower-cost cross-border transactions, serving over 1,000 SMEs and processing more than $32 billion annually. This move signifies continued investor confidence in blockchain-based financial services for emerging economies.

item.title: Chambal Fertilisers Reports 30% Q4 Profit Jump, Shares Cautious
item.description: Chambal Fertilisers and Chemicals Ltd. reported a robust 29.8% year-on-year increase in net profit to ₹169.3 crore for Q4 FY26. This was driven by a 13.7% revenue jump and significant EBITDA margin expansion to 9.2%. The board recommended a ₹6 per share final dividend. Despite the strong results, the company's shares saw a slight dip on May 14, closing down 0.59% at ₹426.40, as investors focus on future sector challenges.

item.title: Voltas Profit Falls 52% As Margins Shrink; Stock Rises
item.description: Voltas Limited reported a 51.8% drop in Q4 FY26 net profit to ₹116.2 crore, even as revenue grew 2.5% to ₹4,887.8 crore. EBITDA fell 33.7%, squeezing profit margins to 4.5% from 7% a year ago. The company's board proposed a ₹4 per share dividend, and Voltas shares closed 2.94% higher on the NSE.

item.title: RBI Cancels 150 NBFC Licenses, Hits Delhi and West Bengal Hardest
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has cancelled the operating licenses for 150 Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs). Among the affected firms are 67 from Delhi and 75 from West Bengal. These companies are now barred from conducting any financial business overseen by the RBI.

item.title: Mittal Plans Bharti Airtel Succession, Eyes 50%+ Promoter Stake
item.description: Telecom leader Sunil Bharti Mittal has unveiled a 10-year plan for Bharti Airtel's leadership handover, aiming for its promoter firm, Bharti Telecom, to hold over 50% stake. This strategy will require an estimated ₹1 lakh crore investment and includes plans for greater control in Airtel Africa. Mittal was also reappointed Chairman until 2031.

item.title: Oracle's AI Pivot: Cuts Indian Campus Job Offers
item.description: Oracle has rescinded numerous campus job offers to students at leading Indian engineering institutes, following a global restructuring and significant layoffs. This move is tied to the company's accelerated investment in AI infrastructure, signaling a strategic shift in talent acquisition priorities. While analysts maintain a 'Buy' consensus, the decision introduces risks related to future workforce development and competitive positioning in a rapidly evolving tech job market.

item.title: SIA Profit Plummets, Revenue Soars; Air India Losses Bite
item.description: Singapore Airlines Group reported a sharp net profit drop for FY26, mainly due to the disappearance of a prior-year merger gain and substantial losses from its associate Air India. Despite this, the airline posted record revenue and a strong 39% jump in operating profit, fueled by high passenger demand and cost management. However, Air India's ongoing issues and rising operational costs cloud the outlook.

item.title: Apollo Tyres Profit Soars 241% on Tax Gain, Margins Expand
item.description: Apollo Tyres Ltd. reported a 241% year-on-year increase in fourth-quarter net profit to ₹631 crore, largely due to a ₹574 crore tax benefit from adopting a new tax regime. Revenue rose 14% to ₹7,336 crore, and operating profit (EBITDA) grew 28% to ₹1,069 crore, with EBITDA margins widening to 14.6%. The company proposed a final dividend of ₹2.50 per share, totaling ₹6 for FY26. Shares closed up 1.89% at ₹404 on May 14, 2026. The company also plans to close its Netherlands plant due to European market pressures.

item.title: Fuel Prices Push Indian Firms to Embrace Hybrid Work
item.description: High fuel prices are making Indian companies rethink full-time office returns, with many moving back to hybrid work. Employee pressure, especially from younger workers citing commuting as an 'invisible tax', is a major factor. While tech firms adapt easily, other industries face hurdles as companies balance cost savings with control and local economic impacts.

item.title: Bank of England Softens Stablecoin Rules for UK Digital Race
item.description: The Bank of England is reportedly easing planned restrictions on stablecoin holdings and reserves after industry concerns. Deputy Governor Sarah Breeden suggested exploring alternatives to rules seen as too strict. This shift is key for the UK to compete globally in digital assets, balancing financial safety with fostering innovation.

item.title: Venu Srinivasan's ₹500 Cr Investment Boosts Jana SFB Capital Ratio
item.description: Venu Srinivasan is set to acquire a 9.99% stake in Jana Small Finance Bank for ₹500 crore through his entity, TVS Venu Management. This investment will increase Jana SFB's capital adequacy ratio by roughly 2.5 percentage points to around 22%, helping address a recent dip. The investment is purely financial and adheres to regulatory limits, with no board seat for Srinivasan, indicating a strategic capital move.

item.title: SIA Profit Plummets 57% Amidst Soaring Air India Losses
item.description: Singapore Airlines Group (SIA) posted a 57.4% net profit drop to SGD 1.184 billion for the fiscal year ending March 2026. This decline, despite a 5% revenue increase to SGD 20.552 billion, was primarily driven by the absence of a significant accounting gain from the Vistara merger and a substantial share of Air India's record $2.8 billion loss. SIA remains committed to its 25.1% investment in Air India, viewing it as a strategic imperative for its multi-hub strategy.

item.title: Bajaj Group Invests ₹2,500 Cr to Build New Integrated Healthcare Ecosystem
item.description: The Bajaj Group is investing over ₹2,500 crore to build a comprehensive healthcare ecosystem starting in Pune, with plans to expand to multiple cities within a decade. Led by Nirav Bajaj, the initiative focuses on an integrated model from prevention to acute care, aiming to deliver services outside traditional hospital settings. This strategic move taps into India's growing healthcare demand, shaped by demographics and rising incomes, while facing industry competition and regulatory hurdles.

item.title: Jewellery Brands Power Mall Revenue, But Create New Risks
item.description: Jewellery retailers are taking up nearly 10% of mall space, becoming vital anchors that drive significant revenue. This growth contrasts with the struggles of some mall operators, like Nexus Select Trust, which has low profitability and a 'Sell' rating. Meanwhile, Kalyan Jewellers is facing a market correction due to regulatory pressures. This trend of relying on a few major tenants could lead to a divided retail market with big differences in value between brands and property owners.

item.title: Crypto Sinks on Inflation Fears Before Key CLARITY Act Vote
item.description: Crypto markets saw widespread selling as traders weighed rising inflation data and the upcoming U.S. CLARITY Act committee vote. Bitcoin fell below $80,000, with Ethereum and other major digital assets also declining. The CLARITY Act aims to clarify U.S. digital asset rules, defining roles for the SEC and CFTC, which could attract institutional investment and shape global regulations. Analysts estimate a 60-65% chance the bill will pass the committee.

item.title: Matrimony.com: Q4 Profit Jumps 20%, Annual Drop, Subscriber Dip Worry
item.description: Matrimony.com posted a 20% year-on-year profit increase in Q4 FY26 to ₹9.7 crore, boosted by higher billings. However, full-year FY26 profit after tax fell to ₹34 crore from ₹45 crore in FY25, with revenue also declining. Despite management's forecast for accelerated growth, a year-on-year drop in paid subscriptions signals concerns about underlying momentum.

item.title: ED Freezes ₹526 Crore Deposits in Gameskraft Money Laundering Probe
item.description: Enforcement Directorate froze ₹526 crore in bank deposits and seized assets from online gaming firm Gameskraft. The agency alleges the company ran illegal real-money rummy operations, manipulated geolocation, and used bots. Three founders have been arrested in the money laundering investigation.

item.title: Senores Pharma's Soaring Profits Meet Market Skepticism
item.description: Senores Pharmaceuticals unveiled robust Q4 and full-year FY26 financial results, demonstrating over 100% year-on-year profit growth fueled by its regulated markets segment and strategic acquisitions. Despite this impressive financial uptick and an aggressive expansion strategy including new ventures in the U.S. federal market, the company's stock saw a modest decline on the announcement. This performance disconnect suggests investor caution may be weighing the company's premium valuation and broader industry headwinds against its substantial operational success.

item.title: India Insurers Drive Personalized Protection Amid Sector Shift
item.description: India's insurance sector is rapidly evolving, shifting from uniform policies to highly personalized, embedded protection integrated into daily financial activities. This transformation, driven by simpler designs, digital integration, and data analytics, aims to enhance access, particularly in smaller regions, with embedded insurance poised as a key growth driver. Despite advancements, the industry faces challenges in closing the protection gap and reorienting consumer focus towards risk cover over mere wealth creation.

item.title: DCM Shriram Profit Soars on Tax Reversal, but Operations Face Pressure
item.description: DCM Shriram reported a 107% year-on-year surge in Q4 FY26 net profit to ₹370 crore, largely thanks to a significant tax reversal. Revenue rose 11% to ₹3,193 crore, but operating margins shrank. The Fenesta division struggled with higher input costs and launching new product lines. Despite a ₹1,000-1,500 crore investment pipeline, the stock fell as investors worried about underlying profitability and margin stability.

item.title: Global Health Profit Jumps 42% on Revenue Surge, Margins Contract
item.description: Global Health Limited reported a 42% surge in fourth-quarter net profit to ₹143.9 crore, boosted by a 24.5% revenue increase to ₹1,159 crore. Despite strong growth, EBITDA margins narrowed to 21% from 24% due to higher costs. The board approved FY26 results, recommended a ₹0.50 per share final dividend, and announced senior management appointments.

item.title: China Seeks US Oil Amid Strait Tensions, Tariffs Pose Hurdle
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and US President Trump discussed China potentially increasing oil purchases from the US, driven by Middle East supply concerns. However, a 20% import tariff and trade friction are major barriers blocking energy deals. Oil prices are around $100-$105 per barrel amid Strait of Hormuz instability.

item.title: US Clarity Act Debate: Coinbase Options Rise on Clarity Hopes, Bitcoin Drops
item.description: The US Clarity Act markup is underway, a key event for digital assets. Bitcoin shows technical weakness, dropping below $80,000 amid concerns over ETF outflows and inflation. Meanwhile, Coinbase (COIN) options markets suggest traders are betting on benefits from regulatory clarity. Despite recent Q1 financial misses, analysts maintain 'Buy' ratings for COIN, though price targets vary. Competitors Riot Platforms and Marathon Digital face significant losses and revenue declines while pivoting to AI data centers.

item.title: India Farmers Face Prices Below Support Levels
item.description: Indian farmers are facing significant economic pressure as market prices for multiple key agricultural commodities, including wheat, paddy, maize, pulses, and oilseeds, are trading below their Minimum Support Prices (MSPs). This situation, exacerbated by slower-than-expected government procurement for the Rabi season and substantial buffer stocks, points to broader sector-wide distress beyond just wheat. State-level procurement shortfalls, particularly in Haryana and Uttar Pradesh, highlight uneven performance in supporting farmers. The economic repercussions include dampened rural demand and potential increases in agricultural non-performing assets.

item.title: SIA Defends S$2.02B Air India Stake Amid Losses: A Risky Bet?
item.description: Singapore Airlines is keeping its S$2.02 billion stake in Air India on its books, signaling confidence in India's long-term growth. However, auditors have highlighted the valuation as a key audit matter due to the high judgment needed to forecast Air India's recovery. This decision comes as SIA's net profit plunged 57.4% for the fiscal year ended March 2026, underscoring risks from substantial capital needs and integration challenges.
